reasoning:
The question asks whether Nodirbek Abdusattorov will have a rating on the resolution dates that is at least 1% higher than his rating on the Wikipedia "FIDE rankings" page as of May 25, 2025. 

On May 1, 2025, FIDE published its monthly rating list, putting his rating at 2771. Wikipedia typically updates these pages monthly, so his Wikipedia rating on May 25, 2025 will be 2771. A 1% increase requires him to reach an official rating of 2771 * 1.01 = 2798.71 (essentially 2799). 

Reaching an Elo of 2799 is historically rare; only a handful of players have ever reached. A jump of ~28 Elo points at the super-GM level requires consistently dominating elite tournaments (scoring +5 or +6 against 2750+ rated players). We know he recently competed in the Grand Chess Tour Superbet Classic Romania in mid-May 2025, where he actually *lost* under five rating points. This means his official June 2025 rating will likely drop to ~2767. 

Therefore:
- On 2025-06-01 and 2025-06-24, his official rating will be the June rating (~2767), making it virtually impossible to resolve YES.
- On 2025-08-23 and 2025-11-21, he has more time, but gaining more than 30 points to reach 2799 within 3-6 months from a ~2766 base remains extremely unlikely. I set the probabilities slightly higher to account for potential breakout streaks, but fundamentally the base rate of hitting 2799 in such a short window is very low.
